title: What's up with WhatsApp?
key_finding: WhatsApp is experienced as informal, social, immediate, and group-friendly compared with SMS.
product_implication: Optimize for casual low-friction replies rather than email-like polish.

title: Everyday Dwelling with WhatsApp
key_finding: WhatsApp maintains ambient closeness and a sense of living alongside others.
product_implication: Recommend phatic check-ins, acknowledgments, and presence-maintaining replies.

title: Sequential Patterns in SMS and WhatsApp Dialogues
key_finding: WhatsApp users split actions across multiple message bubbles more fluidly than SMS.
product_implication: Generate message bundles and adjacency-aware replies, not only one long answer.

title: SMS: Exploiting the Interactional Characteristics of Near-Synchrony
key_finding: SMS succeeds through brief, immediate, phatic, low-threshold contact.
product_implication: Offer lightweight acknowledgments when full replies would feel too heavy.

title: Please Reply!
key_finding: SMS interaction creates reciprocity and reply obligation norms.
product_implication: Model whether a response is socially due before drafting content.

title: y do tngrs luv 2 txt msg?
key_finding: Teen texting supports coordination, privacy, and peer relationship maintenance.
product_implication: Learn relational use cases such as logistics, teasing, intimacy, and privacy.

title: Wan2tlk?: Everyday Text Messaging
key_finding: Texting often supports quick coordination and concise endings.
product_implication: Suggest natural closures such as ok, lmk, see you, and brief confirmations.

title: A Large Scale Study of Text-Messaging Use
key_finding: Large message logs show many contacts, long-running threads, and parallel conversations.
product_implication: Rank suggestions by thread/contact context, not global style alone.

title: Generation Txt?
key_finding: Text language is creative and socially patterned rather than degraded writing.
product_implication: Preserve idiosyncratic spelling, casing, punctuation, and abbreviations as style signals.

title: Interactional Coherence in CMC
key_finding: CMC has disrupted adjacency, overlapping exchanges, and topic decay.
product_implication: Infer which prior bubble is being answered before generating a reply.

title: Turn-Taking in Quasi-Synchronous Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: Typed chat messages arrive as complete postings and differ from spoken turns.
product_implication: Treat each sent bubble as an action unit rather than a speech-like turn.

title: Repair in Chats
key_finding: Chat users adapt repair practices to typed quasi-synchronous media.
product_implication: Generate natural correction options such as wait I meant or sorry typo.

title: Repair: Comparing Facebook chat with spoken interaction
key_finding: Written chat has construction-time and sequential repair behavior.
product_implication: Detect correction contexts and suggest self-repair separately from main replies.

title: Conversation Analysis and Online Interaction
key_finding: Digital conversation analysis must account for platform affordances and sequence.
product_implication: Represent read receipts, typing, reactions, media, sequence, and platform constraints.

title: Hahaha: Laughter as a Resource to Manage WhatsApp Conversations
key_finding: Written laughter does different interactional work depending on position.
product_implication: Learn laughter placement as standalone, preface, response, or repair buffer.

title: A Study of Phatic Emoji Use in WhatsApp Communication
key_finding: Emoji norms vary by relationship and solidarity.
product_implication: Personalize emoji by relationship type rather than user-wide averages only.

title: Stance Taking with Laugh Particles and Emojis
key_finding: Laugh particles and emojis mark stance in WhatsApp chats.
product_implication: Distinguish amused, affiliative, awkward, and softening laughter.

title: Interaction and Outeraction: Instant Messaging in Action
key_finding: IM supports informal coordination and availability management.
product_implication: Recommend availability probes such as quick q, you around, or can I call.

title: Instant Messaging in the Workplace
key_finding: Workplace IM can include complex, task-focused discussions.
product_implication: Keep task-state memory separate from tone imitation.

title: Responsiveness in Instant Messaging
key_finding: Response timing can be predicted from contextual and user signals.
product_implication: Recommend urgency and timing, not just wording.

title: I'm Waiting: Timing and Responsiveness in Semi-Synchronous Communication
key_finding: Semi-synchronous channels create expectations around waiting and availability.
product_implication: Offer reply-now, acknowledge-now, and reply-later options.

title: Online Pauses and Silence
key_finding: Response latency affects credibility, attractiveness, and evaluation.
product_implication: Treat silence as meaningful and suggest delay-aware repairs.

title: Pauses and Response Latencies in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: CMC latency carries social information, not just technical delay.
product_implication: Add timing distributions to persona profiles.

title: Universals and Cultural Variation in Turn-Taking
key_finding: Conversation minimizes overlap and silence with cultural variation.
product_implication: Calibrate cadence by relationship, culture, and locale instead of assuming instant replies.

title: Timing in Turn-Taking and Its Implications for Processing Models
key_finding: Human turn-taking is fast because comprehension and production overlap.
product_implication: Precompute likely response intents for live drafting surfaces.

title: Inter-Turn Silence in Conversation
key_finding: Silent gaps are socially interpreted differently across languages and cultures.
product_implication: Use relationship-specific and culture-specific late-reply thresholds.

title: Expressing Emotion in Text-Based Communication
key_finding: Emotion is signaled through punctuation, verbosity, affect words, and disagreement.
product_implication: Treat punctuation and length as affective style controls.

title: Emoticons in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: Emoticon use depends on valence, relationship, and social motive.
product_implication: Vary emoji/emoticon behavior by close friend, family, date, or coworker.

title: Computer-Mediated Communication: Impersonal, Interpersonal, and Hyperpersonal Interaction
key_finding: CMC can amplify selective self-presentation and hyperpersonal effects.
product_implication: Avoid over-idealized AI polish; preserve plausible imperfections.

title: Smart Reply: Automated Response Suggestion for Email
key_finding: Short diverse response suggestions work at scale.
product_implication: Offer distinct concise candidates such as accept, defer, and ask-follow-up.

title: AI-Mediated Communication: Definition, Research Agenda, and Ethical Considerations
key_finding: AI-mediated communication changes agency, mediation, ethics, and interpersonal goals.
product_implication: Make outputs assistive, editable, and clearly human-reviewed.

title: AI-Mediated Communication: Profile Text Written by AI Affects Trustworthiness
key_finding: Perceived AI authorship can affect trustworthiness.
product_implication: Preserve user authorship signals and avoid hidden impersonation.

title: AI-Mediated Communication: Language Use and Interpersonal Effects
key_finding: AI suggestions can bias language and interpersonal perception.
product_implication: Audit positivity drift and persona homogenization.

title: AI in Communication Impacts Language and Social Relationships
key_finding: Algorithmic replies increase speed, positive language, and perceived cooperation.
product_implication: Do not flatten conflict, sarcasm, boundaries, or reluctance into generic positivity.

title: Will AI Console Me when I Lose my Pet?
key_finding: Trust changes when recipients know AI helped craft intimate messages.
product_implication: For sensitive contexts, suggest fragments or prompts rather than send-ready prose.

title: Sometimes I'll Start a Sentence in Spanish y termino en espanol
key_finding: Classic typology separates discourse, sentence, constituent, and tag switching.
product_implication: Label switch type instead of storing a binary code-switch flag.

title: Matrix Language Frame Model
key_finding: The matrix language supplies grammatical structure for embedded language material.
product_implication: Constrain generation by matrix language and embedded token placement.

title: Bilingual Speech: A Typology of Code-Mixing
key_finding: Code-mixing patterns include insertion, alternation, and congruent lexicalization.
product_implication: Preserve participant-specific switch pattern ratios.

title: A Conversation Analytic Model of Code-Switching
key_finding: Switches organize footing, topic, seriousness, and interactional moves.
product_implication: Switch at discourse functions such as joke, repair, emphasis, or intimacy.

title: Code-Switching and the Internet
key_finding: Internet communication is a normal site for code-switching.
product_implication: Treat chat code-switching as style, not noise or bad grammar.

title: Code-Switching in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: CMC code-switching varies by mode, audience, and identity.
product_implication: Condition switching on chat, relationship, and group context.

title: Code-Switching in WhatsApp and Viber Messages
key_finding: Digital chat switching can be syntactic and topic motivated.
product_implication: Extract topic windows and syntactic positions around switches.

title: Why Code-Switch on WhatsApp?
key_finding: WhatsApp university chats show functional code-switching.
product_implication: Track functions such as humor, emphasis, identity, efficiency, and quotation.

title: Predicting Language Choice in a Digital Medium
key_finding: WhatsApp lexical choice can be predicted from linguistic and social features.
product_implication: Use retrieval from same relationship/context for code-switch examples.

title: Code-Switching in WhatsApp Parent/Student Chats
key_finding: WhatsApp chats include tag, intra-sentential, and inter-sentential switching.
product_implication: Do not collapse family, school, work, and friend switch profiles.

title: The Emergence of Hinglish
key_finding: Hinglish is socially widespread in online and offline communication.
product_implication: Do not normalize Hinglish into pure English or pure Hindi.

title: Code-Mixing in Social Media Text: The Last Language Identification Frontier?
key_finding: Language ID struggles with code-mixing, borrowing, and phonetic typing.
product_implication: Use token-level language ID with uncertainty, romanized lexicons, and raw text preservation.

title: Code Mixing: A Challenge for Language Identification in the Language of Social Media
key_finding: Bengali, Hindi, and English social media mixing complicates LID.
product_implication: Label names, URLs, emoji, and universal tokens separately from language switches.

title: Overview for the First Shared Task on Language Identification in Code-Switched Data
key_finding: Word-level language ID was formalized for code-switched data.
product_implication: Add LID as a first-pass annotation layer for generation audits.

title: Overview for the Second Shared Task on Language Identification in Code-Switched Data
key_finding: Language ID remains hard for closely related varieties and noisy data.
product_implication: Store ambiguous labels and confidence rather than forcing hard language tags.

title: A Hindi-English Code-Switched Corpus
key_finding: ICON/CanVEC includes Facebook, Twitter, and WhatsApp with language/POS tags.
product_implication: Reuse token language and POS annotation ideas for WhatsApp exports.

title: POS Tagging of English-Hindi Code-Mixed Social Media Content
key_finding: Hindi-English social text needs transliteration, normalization, and POS handling.
product_implication: Keep raw romanization canonical and normalized shadows only for analysis.

title: LinCE: A Centralized Benchmark for Linguistic Code-Switching Evaluation
key_finding: Benchmark covers LID, NER, POS, and sentiment across code-switched pairs.
product_implication: Evaluate code-switching with multiple tasks instead of BLEU alone.

title: GLUECoS: An Evaluation Benchmark for Code-Switched NLP
key_finding: English-Hindi benchmark spans LID, POS, NER, sentiment, QA, and NLI.
product_implication: Benchmark prompt-only against code-switch-specialized baselines.

title: SentiMix 2020
key_finding: Hinglish data can combine word-level LID with sentiment labels.
product_implication: Track affective context around switches and particles.

title: PHINC: A Parallel Hinglish Social Media Code-Mixed Corpus
key_finding: Hinglish translation remains hard and original code-mixed text matters.
product_implication: Never train only on translated text if WhatsApp style is the target.

title: hinglishNorm: Human-Normalized Hinglish Corpus
key_finding: Noisy romanized Hinglish benefits from raw and normalized representations.
product_implication: Store raw text plus normalized analysis fields and spelling variant stats.

title: L3Cube-HingCorpus and Hinglish Language Models
key_finding: Large Roman Hindi-English corpora enable HingBERT/HingGPT-style models.
product_implication: Consider Hinglish-specific models or adapters when mixed-language fidelity matters.

title: COMI-LINGUA: Code-Mixed Linguistic Annotations
key_finding: Hindi-English annotations include matrix language, LID, POS, NER, and MT.
product_implication: Use token LID, matrix language, NER, POS, and translation shadows in the schema.

title: Matrix Language Detection in Code-Switched Text
key_finding: Matrix language can be modeled explicitly from linguistic theory.
product_implication: Compute matrix language per message and expose confidence.

title: Joint Part-of-Speech and Language ID Tagging for Code-Switched Data
key_finding: Matrix language assumptions do not always converge neatly.
product_implication: Allow mixed or unclear matrix language when confidence is low.

title: Comparing the Level of Code-Switching in Corpora
key_finding: Code-Mixing Index compares mixing complexity across corpora.
product_implication: Match CMI bands and switch-point distributions rather than averages only.

title: Limits of Code-Mixing Metrics
key_finding: CMI-style metrics miss readability, semantics, and noisy-text quality.
product_implication: Pair CMI with semantic preservation and naturalness filters.

title: Language Modeling for Code-Mixing: The Role of Linguistic Theory Based Synthetic Data
key_finding: Theory-compatible synthetic code-mixing improves language modeling.
product_implication: Use synthetic code-switch examples only as labeled augmentation, not human ground truth.

title: LLM Code-Switch Generation Is Inconsistent
key_finding: Large models can be inconsistent on zero-shot code-switch generation.
product_implication: Human-review generated Hinglish before training or evaluation.

title: Code-Switching with Multilingual LLMs
key_finding: Multilingual LLMs can underperform smaller fine-tuned models on code-switched tasks.
product_implication: Prefer retrieval or adapters when code-switch fidelity matters.

title: Code-Switched Translation Evaluation with LLMs
key_finding: Code-switched translation needs direct evaluation.
product_implication: Separate meaning preservation from style-switch fidelity.

title: Fine-Tuning for Natural Code-Switch Generation
key_finding: Natural code-switch-derived pairs improve generation but automatic metrics are weak.
product_implication: Evaluate with human preference plus token-level diagnostics.

title: Lost in the Mix: Evaluating LLMs on Code-Switched Inputs
key_finding: Code-switched inputs can degrade LLM comprehension asymmetrically.
product_implication: Add held-out comprehension tests for mixed-language contexts.

title: CodeMixQA
key_finding: CodeMixQA evaluates understanding, reasoning, and generation across scripts and transliteration.
product_implication: Test original-script and romanized variants before launch.

title: Emoticons and Illocutionary Force
key_finding: Emoticons can mark emotion, convention, and illocutionary force.
product_implication: Tag emoji by pragmatic function, not only sentiment.

title: Emoticon, Emoji, and Sticker Use in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: Graphicons affect relationships, understanding, and interaction.
product_implication: Use a graphicon taxonomy for emoji, stickers, GIFs, images, and videos.

title: Graphicons in CMC
key_finding: Emoji, stickers, GIFs, images, and videos perform distinct communicative functions.
product_implication: Extract graphicon frequency and function tags such as reaction, riffing, action, and narrative.

title: LINE Sticker Use
key_finding: Stickers carry valence and relationship-sensitive placement.
product_implication: Insert media placeholders only when intimacy and affect support it.

title: The Perfect One: GIFs and Relationship Context
key_finding: GIF meaning depends on source context and shared knowledge.
product_implication: Tag GIF/media placeholders with intent and relationship context.

title: Using Millions of Emoji Occurrences to Learn Any-Domain Representations
key_finding: Emoji prediction can transfer emotion and sarcasm signals.
product_implication: Use held-out emoji recovery as an auxiliary style evaluation.

title: emoji2vec
key_finding: Emoji can be embedded from Unicode descriptions and context.
product_implication: Store raw emoji and semantic descriptions for rare emoji generalization.

title: EmojiNet
key_finding: Emoji senses can be inventoried and disambiguated.
product_implication: Map ambiguous emoji to context-specific candidate senses.

title: SemEval 2018 Task 2: Multilingual Emoji Prediction
key_finding: Emoji prediction can be framed as context-to-emoji classification.
product_implication: Use emoji prediction as a benchmark shape for graphicon modeling.

title: Linguistic Purpose of Emoji
key_finding: Emoji can function as content, function, multimodal supplement, or redundancy.
product_implication: Classify emoji function and evaluate with human agreement.

title: Understanding Emoji Ambiguity in Context
key_finding: Emoji interpretation can be ambiguous and platform-sensitive.
product_implication: Keep raw Unicode and platform/rendering metadata when available.

title: Emoji Ambiguity Across Platforms
key_finding: Users interpret the same emoji differently across platforms.
product_implication: Do not assume one universal emoji meaning.

title: Non-Face Emoji and Affect
key_finding: Non-face emoji convey affect and disambiguate text.
product_implication: Do not collapse emoji modeling to face sentiment buckets.

title: Ellipsis as a Discourse Marker
key_finding: Ellipses can function as discourse markers in digital text.
product_implication: Extract ellipsis count and position as style tags.

title: Texting Insincerely: The Role of the Period
key_finding: Final periods in short texts can signal abruptness or insincerity.
product_implication: Tag terminal periods separately from sentence punctuation.

title: Punctuation in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: Repeated punctuation can change perceived warmth, competence, and affect.
product_implication: Use repeated punctuation only in measured relationship-specific bursts.

title: Caps Lock and Typography as Prosody
key_finding: Capitalization and typography can mark prosodic emphasis.
product_implication: Tag all-caps ratio and scope as emphasis, not simply shouting.

title: Letter Repetition in Computer-Mediated Communication
key_finding: Repeated letters carry expressive meaning in CMC.
product_implication: Store root and elongated surface for re-realization.

title: Cooooooooollllllllllllll!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
key_finding: Orthographic elongation signals sentiment and intensity.
product_implication: Use elongation as an affective tag and evaluate recovery after normalization.

title: Written Laughter and CMC Cues
key_finding: Laughter, punctuation, elongation, and spelling are manipulable CMC cues.
product_implication: Test generation with ablations of these cues.

title: Haha and Laughter in Online Interaction
key_finding: Laughter position can mark stance and interactional function.
product_implication: Tag initial, final, standalone, and repair laughter separately.

title: Humor Markers in Online Communication
key_finding: Humor markers include emoticons, punctuation, laughter, formatting, and elongation.
product_implication: Detect humor as a bundle of cues, not a single token.

title: Textisms and Literacy
key_finding: CMC representation can encode textisms, abbreviations, and nonstandard spelling.
product_implication: Use tags for abbreviations, homophones, clippings, and omitted punctuation.

title: SMS Text Messaging Style
key_finding: SMS style uses emoticons, abbreviations, letter homophones, and functional orientation.
product_implication: Evaluate style by social function rather than spelling correctness only.

title: CMC Pragmatics
key_finding: Particles and backchannels are pragmatic phenomena in sequential context.
product_implication: Model particles/backchannels as conversation-flow tags.

title: Low-Resource Authorship Style Transfer: Can Non-Famous Authors Be Imitated?
key_finding: Author imitation from only about 16 posts is challenging and current methods do not master it.
product_implication: Use prompt cards first and keep confidence low for tiny samples.

title: TinyStyler: Efficient Few-Shot Text Style Transfer with Authorship Embeddings
key_finding: Small models with authorship embeddings can outperform strong LLM prompting for few-shot style transfer.
product_implication: Consider small controllable stylizers before full fine-tuning.

title: LLMs Still Struggle to Imitate the Implicit Writing Styles of Everyday Authors
key_finding: LLMs struggle with nuanced informal writing such as blogs and forums even with examples.
product_implication: Do not trust prompt confidence for WhatsApp; require held-out human checks.

title: Learning to Generate Text in Arbitrary Writing Styles
key_finding: Stylometric representations can guide generation toward target writing styles.
product_implication: Use explicit style metrics and retrieval-guided decoding before model training.

title: STRAP: Style Transfer via Paraphrasing
key_finding: Unsupervised style transfer can be framed as paraphrase generation.
product_implication: Generate variants by preserving intent and restyling, then filter for drift.

title: Style Transfer Through Back-Translation
key_finding: Back-translation can preserve meaning while changing style.
product_implication: Use backtranslation as one augmentation source, not sole ground truth.

title: Controllable Text Generation for Few-Shot Style Transfer
key_finding: Few-shot control can learn style differences from limited examples.
product_implication: Learn how style changes wording rather than whole-person mimicry.

title: Polite Dialogue Generation Without Parallel Data
key_finding: Weak supervision can steer coarse dialogue style attributes.
product_implication: Use classifiers for coarse dimensions such as formality and warmth.

title: Personalizing Dialogue Agents: Persona-Based Neural Conversation Model
key_finding: Persona embeddings improve speaker consistency in dialogue.
product_implication: Keep a separate persona/style representation instead of only raw examples.

title: Persona-Chat: Personalized Dialogue
key_finding: Explicit profile conditioning improves engaging and specific dialogue.
product_implication: Use consented style/persona cards and avoid mining private facts as style.

title: Self-Instruct
key_finding: Synthetic instruction data can be bootstrapped then filtered and deduplicated.
product_implication: Use generate-filter-dedupe loops for reply intents and preference prompts.

title: Baize: An Open-Source Chat Model with Parameter-Efficient Tuning on Self-Chat Data
key_finding: Self-chat plus parameter-efficient tuning can create multi-turn chat data.
product_implication: Synthetic dialogue can help but must be labeled and guardrailed.

title: EDA: Easy Data Augmentation Techniques for Boosting Performance on Text Classification Tasks
key_finding: Simple augmentation helps small classification datasets.
product_implication: Use conservative edits for classifiers, not as final persona messages.

title: AEDA: An Easier Data Augmentation Technique for Text Classification
key_finding: Punctuation insertion can preserve content while augmenting classifiers.
product_implication: Use for robustness checks, not for style ground truth.

title: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
key_finding: LoRA trains low-rank adapter matrices while freezing base weights.
product_implication: Store per-person style in removable adapters.

title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
key_finding: 4-bit quantized LoRA enables efficient fine-tuning on modest hardware.
product_implication: Use QLoRA after hundreds of clean examples, not tiny raw exports.

title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
key_finding: Small learned prefixes can adapt frozen models in low-data regimes.
product_implication: Consider soft prompts for low-risk per-person routing.

title: The Power of Scale for Parameter-Efficient Prompt Tuning
key_finding: Frozen models plus learned prompts can use labeled examples efficiently.
product_implication: Use prompt tuning where adapter management is heavy.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP
key_finding: Non-parametric memory improves specificity and updateability.
product_implication: Keep memories retrievable and deletable rather than baked into style adapters.

title: Generalization through Memorization: Nearest Neighbor Language Models
key_finding: kNN retrieval adapts language models without additional training.
product_implication: Use nearest-neighbor examples to ground style while auditing memorized emission.

title: Direct Preference Optimization
key_finding: Preference optimization can use chosen/rejected pairs without a reward model.
product_implication: Use DPO for target-style vs generic/private/overcopied rejected replies.

title: KTO: Model Alignment as Prospect Theoretic Optimization
key_finding: KTO can learn from binary desirable/undesirable feedback.
product_implication: Use KTO only after enough thumbs-up/down labels exist.

title: ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model
key_finding: ORPO combines supervised learning with preference contrast in one stage.
product_implication: Try after SFT/DPO baselines for reviewed preference data.

title: The Secret Sharer: Evaluating and Testing Unintended Memorization
key_finding: Language models can unintentionally memorize rare training sequences.
product_implication: Add canaries and duplicate/near-duplicate leakage tests.

title: Extracting Training Data from Large Language Models
key_finding: Models can emit memorized training data, including private text.
product_implication: Do not train raw private chats without consent, redaction, and leakage audits.
